name: Doomedag
db_description:
https://dommedag.netlify.app/
https://github.com/uset82/iFoundYou

No es solo un localizador. Es una plataforma de alertas y comunicación creada para ayudar a las personas a estar más preparadas ante situaciones inusuales.

La aplicación ya cuenta con un sistema de alarma que detecta movimientos aéreos inusuales. Además, el proyecto puede crecer con hardware para permitir comunicación directa entre personas, incluso sin acceso a internet.

Está abierto para todos. Puedes hacer fork, modificarlo, agregar herramientas o colaborar con pull requests.

Si te interesa, eres bienvenido a contribuir y mejorarlo.

analysis.summary: iFoundYou (branded "Dommedag") is a real-time location-sharing, emergency-alerts, and offline-mesh-communications PWA with a React Native mobile companion. It uses Supabase for auth, database (PostGIS), and realtime subscriptions; Netlify Functions for serverless API; and integrates Meshtastic BLE/HTTP hardware for offline mesh networking. A gateway mode bridges Meshtastic mesh messages into Supabase community alerts — directly useful in disaster scenarios where some users have internet and others do not. The codebase is substantive, not boilerplate, but suffers from a 2357-line god-component App.tsx, no CI, and a single trivial test.
analysis.project_type: Full-stack web PWA + React Native mobile — emergency location-sharing and mesh communications platform

architecture.orm_or_db_layer: Supabase JS client (supabase-js) used directly — no ORM. Raw PostGIS SQL for spatial queries. IndexedDB via idb library for offline cache.
architecture.api_design: Supabase REST + Realtime for main data; Netlify Functions (nearby.js, community-alerts.js, discover.js, alerts.js) for geospatial queries requiring server-side auth and business logic
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Library code well-separated in web/src/lib/{chat,mesh,EWS}. However, App.tsx is a 2357-line god component containing all UI state, effects, and business logic — a significant violation of separation of concerns.
architecture.modularity: Transport abstraction (MeshTransport interface with BluetoothMeshTransport and HttpMeshTransport implementations) is well-designed. Chat layer has TransportManager, offlineSync, messageStore, realtimeChat as distinct modules. BridgeManager handles mesh-to-internet relay independently.
notable_patterns: Offline-first outbox queue: optimistic writes to IndexedDB, drain on reconnect
Transport abstraction layer with reconnect logic and exponential backoff (BLE transport)
Gateway mode: elected users bridge Meshtastic mesh messages to Supabase community alerts
Supabase RLS policies on all 11 tables with spatial-aware location policies
PostGIS geography columns with spatial GiST indexes for proximity queries
match_contacts() security-definer SQL function for contact discovery without exposing PII

code_organization.directory_structure: Clean top-level split into web/, mobile/, netlify/, supabase/, mcp/, wifi/. web/src/lib is well-organized into chat/ and mesh/ sub-modules. Root directory is cluttered with 8+ research .md files that are not production artifacts.
code_organization.naming_quality: Good — consistent camelCase TypeScript, descriptive file names (realtimeChat.ts, offlineSync.ts, BluetoothMeshTransport.ts). Netlify function names match their purpose. SQL files have descriptive names.
code_organization.documentation_quality: AGENTS.md, CONTINUITY.md, and a docs/ directory show active planning documentation. In-code JSDoc comments present on key modules with phase tracking (Phase 1.3, 4.4, etc.). README present but not audited in detail.
code_organization.reasoning: The library layer is genuinely well-organized, but App.tsx at 2357 lines is a structural liability — all state, all effects, and most business logic are co-located in one file. Research markdown files in the repo root indicate fast iteration without housekeeping.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: Secrets via environment variables: VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY for the web frontend; SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY for Netlify Functions. No hardcoded secrets detected. A hasSupabaseConfig guard prevents crashes when env vars are missing.
production_readiness.security_notes: RLS enabled on all 11 database tables with well-crafted policies (owner-only writes, friend-visibility reads, discoverable flag for strangers). match_contacts() uses SECURITY DEFINER with explicit REVOKE/GRANT to prevent PII leakage. Netlify Functions validate JWT tokens against Supabase /auth/v1/user before processing. No rate limiting on any serverless endpoint — a risk if deployed under load.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: PostGIS GiST spatial indexes on geom columns in last_locations and community_alerts. Supabase Realtime handles push for chat. Netlify Functions scale horizontally by default. Location updates throttled client-side to 15s intervals. No server-side caching or rate limits.
production_readiness.reasoning: Auth and RLS are solid — this is the strongest aspect. Deploy configs exist for both Netlify and Render. Error states are surfaced to the UI. However, structured logging is absent (only console.log/warn/error), no rate limiting on serverless functions, and the BridgeManager inserts lat:0/lon:0 for mesh messages without location data — a data quality bug.

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: Real, substantive implementation. The offline outbox queue, Meshtastic transport abstraction with exponential-backoff reconnect, PostGIS schema with spatial indexes, and Supabase RLS policies are all genuine, working logic — not generated scaffolding.
maturity.reasoning: 47 commits with activity through May 2026 shows sustained development. Tests consist of a single trivial React Native snapshot render with no assertions, which provides no real coverage. No CI/CD pipeline exists (.github directory absent), meaning nothing validates builds or tests on push.

viability.reasoning: The core feature set — friend location sharing, geo-tagged community emergency alerts, and Meshtastic mesh networking with internet bridge — maps directly onto earthquake disaster needs (finding people, broadcasting resource needs, communicating when internet is down). The PostGIS spatial queries and offline outbox are production-grade. Main blockers to deployment are: the 2357-line god-component App.tsx needs splitting before sustained contribution is viable, no CI makes regressions risky, and the app requires manual Supabase project setup. The app is branded 'Dommedag' (Norwegian: Doomsday) and is not Venezuela-specific, though the functionality is applicable.

key_features: Real-time GPS location sharing with friends (PostGIS + Supabase Realtime)
Geo-tagged community emergency alerts with category filtering (water/food/medical/shelter/lost)
Meshtastic BLE and HTTP mesh transport for offline communications
Internet-to-mesh and mesh-to-internet gateway bridge mode
Offline-first P2P chat with IndexedDB outbox queue and automatic drain on reconnect
Contact discovery by email/phone (match_contacts security-definer function)
Proximity push notifications (Web Notifications API + cooldown)
QR code invite system for friend pairing
EWS (Early Warning System) panel with aircraft overlay on MapLibre map
PWA with iOS/Android install prompts and service worker
React Native mobile app with native iOS multipeer connectivity (Swift)
WiFi scanner Python module (cross-platform) for network discovery
analysis.merge_potential: Medium. The offline mesh bridge and geo-tagged community alerts are directly deployable for earthquake response and could integrate with shelter-mapping or aid-logistics tools. The PostGIS spatial layer and RLS-secured schema are reusable. Would require: splitting App.tsx, adding CI, configuring a Supabase project, and localizing the UI to Spanish.
red_flags: App.tsx is 2357 lines — all state management, effects, and UI in one god component, making contribution and maintenance fragile
No CI/CD pipeline — no automated build or test validation on push
Single trivial mobile snapshot test with no assertions — effectively zero test coverage
BridgeManager inserts lat:0/lon:0 into community_alerts for mesh messages without embedded location — creates corrupt geospatial data
No rate limiting on any Netlify Function endpoint — vulnerable to alert spam or location-flooding
Hardcoded production Netlify URL (imaginative-rolypoly-7fda4b.netlify.app) inside App.tsx shareApp function
WiFi scanner module (wifi/mac_spoofer.py) includes MAC address spoofing — potential legal/ethical concern in deployment contexts
8+ research markdown files in repo root (do deep reseach about Loopt app..., googlereseach2.md, etc.) are noise that indicate fast-and-loose development hygiene
App is branded Dommedag (Norwegian for Doomsday) with no localization to Spanish — requires i18n work before Venezuela deployment

evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: The feature set maps directly onto earthquake disaster needs: real-time GPS location sharing helps families find each other after displacement; geo-tagged community alerts (water/food/medical/shelter/lost categories) broadcast resource needs; the Meshtastic mesh networking bridge lets people communicate even when cell infrastructure is down — exactly the scenario that hits Venezuela after a major quake. The offline-first outbox queue and internet-to-mesh gateway are genuinely disaster-relevant, not just theoretical.
evaluation.live_demo_notes: The page at https://dommedag.netlify.app/ loads and returns an HTML title ("Dommedag — Mesh Guardian") but WebFetch could not confirm any functional UI — it is a React SPA so full rendering requires a real browser with JavaScript. The page does not appear to be broken at the network level, but no interactive elements, map, or login screen were confirmed visible. A real user would need to open it in a browser to see the actual PWA shell.

diffusion.recommended_angle: If promoted at all, lead with the offline mesh networking angle — 'communicates without internet via Meshtastic radios' is a genuinely unique differentiator — and pair it with a short screen-recording showing the map and alert categories. Do not promote before Spanish localization and a working demo walkthrough exist.
gaps: Branding is 'Dommedag' (Norwegian for Doomsday) — must be localized to Spanish before Venezuela deployment
Live demo shows only a title in automated checks — no visible working flow for a promoter to reference
No license — contributors and NGOs cannot legally fork/deploy
UI language appears to be English, not Spanish — a barrier for Venezuelan users
MAC address spoofing module raises legal/ethical concerns that complicate public promotion

evaluation.one_line_pitch: Dommedag lets earthquake survivors share GPS locations, broadcast emergency alerts, and chat over Meshtastic radios — even when the internet is down.
